info.name: Amazon EFS Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) API

params.name: CreationToken
params.description: Restricts the list to the file system with this creation token.

params.name: FileSystemId
params.description: The ID of the file system to describe.

params.name: MaxItems
params.description: The maximum number of file systems to return.

params.name: Marker
params.description: An opaque pagination token returned from a previous request.

items.docs: Returns the description of a specific Amazon EFS file system or all file systems owned by the caller.
Creates a new, empty file system. The operation requires a creation token in the request that Amazon EFS uses to ensure idempotent creation.

params.name: FileSystemId
params.description: The ID of the file system to delete.

items.docs: Deletes a file system, permanently severing access to its contents. You must delete all mount targets associated with the file system before you can delete it.

params.name: FileSystemId
params.description: The ID of the file system whose mount targets to list.

params.name: MountTargetId
params.description: The ID of the mount target to describe.

params.name: MaxItems
params.description: The maximum number of mount targets to return.

params.name: Marker
params.description: An opaque pagination token returned from a previous request.

items.docs: Returns the descriptions of all the current mount targets for a file system.
Creates a mount target for a file system. You can then mount the file system on EC2 instances by using the mount target.
